offensive_abilities.critical_failure: You fail to blast any creatures with the burning exhaust. The sand racer moves its Speed in a straight line and becomes uncontrolled.
offensive_abilities.critical_success: The sand racer moves up to its Speed and can turn normally. Choose up to two creatures that the sand racer was adjacent to when it began this movement. Both of those creatures take 6d6 fire damage from the sand racer's burning exhaust (DC 30 basic Reflex save).
offensive_abilities.failure: As success, but the creature gains a +4 circumstance bonus to its Reflex save, and the sand racer moves its Speed in a straight line.
offensive_abilities.name: Burning Exhaust
offensive_abilities.success: As critical success, but choose only one creature.
offensive_abilities.text: Attempt a two-action Piloting check to Drive the sand racer.

offensive_abilities.name: Exploratory Kit
offensive_abilities.text: Sand racers were built to aid in long-distance exploration of Akiton's sandy wastelands, and have storage capacity for 14 days of rations for a Medium creature and 14 days of fuel. They also have a small storage compartment in the front designed to store a repair kit and replacement parts for the more delicate pieces of machinery.

offensive_abilities.effect: The pilot triggers an injection of thasteron directly into the sand racer's engine to briefly increase its speed. For 1 round the sand racer's Speed becomes 180 feet, all piloting checks with the sand racer gain the reckless trait, and the pilot gains a +2 circumstance to any Acrobatics or piloting checks made to navigate specific challenges. A speed boost is terribly inefficient, and consumes a day's worth of fuel.
offensive_abilities.name: Speed Boost

statistics.space: 10 feet long, 5 feet wide, 5 feet high

traits.name: Uncommon
traits.text: Something of uncommon rarity requires special training or comes from a particular culture or part of the world. Some character choices give access to uncommon options, and the GM can choose to allow access for anyone. Less is known about uncommon creatures than common creatures. They typically can't be summoned. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to these creature is increased by 2.

traits.name: Magical
traits.text: Something with the magical trait is imbued with magical energies not tied to a specific tradition of magic. Some items or effects are closely tied to a particular tradition of magic. In these cases, the item has the arcane, divine, occult, or primal trait instead of the magical trait. Any of these traits indicate that the item is magical.
